item.title: My small head hurts.
item.content:encoded: I did not expect this to be this good. Wow, just… wow. Kaktus, you madlad, you've done it again.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
i was literally about to make a post asking your exact question; i don't know if kaktus has a specific reason for leaving the document at clearance level 5, but since O5-1 specifically has final say over permitting access to it, it would seem like level 6 would be more accurate according to the acs.

i guess something more like "The following file is Level 6 Classified, with esoteric conditions" was what i was imagining.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Simply one of the best on the site. No words to describe just how good this is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is amazing. A one-of-a-kind concept that gets to the point and pulls it off well. Great job, certainly deserves more than 938 upvotes!

Edit: I have a little question. If only 7 people are allowed access to this file, why isn’t this Cosmic Top Secret?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is the only time i've enjoyed the writing of DJkaktus. Very well done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Only Djkaktus could make something this good make something this great out of literally nothing.

Most definitely upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just found this for the first time, and I really loved it. I had a couple ideas about potential tests/logs, if you'd like to hear them or give me permission to add them to your article/create new article pages for them, I'd really appreciate it. If not I get it, it's nice to have your own work unaltered by anyone other than you. I respect the creativity and writing ability!

item.title: Re: So.
item.content:encoded: I think people who have died in the mean time still count as knowing about it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is one that I literally lie awake in bed thinking about. Staring at the ceiling. Wondering what vast emptinesses my mind substitutes existences for.

The exploration log makes me extremely uncomfortable as well. Everything about it just makes my skin crawl.

So excellent.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is that much worse if your days tend to be repetitive anyway—what if everything around you is just your brain papering over a void in an unimaginative fashion?

And now I have that something's-right-behind-you feeling, only it's nothing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Absolutely loved it, well done. Exploration log gave me chills at some point. Interview didn't fit so well tho. But +1 anyway.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I just noticed that the plaque has been updated, now citing Calvin Lucien as Director and Site-01 being responsible for this SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is honestly, in my opinion, one of the most creative and masterfully crafted articles on the site. The exploration log with the D-Class in particular elicits from me a sense of creeping dread that no other article on the site has managed to achieve. By far one of the best articles on here.

item.title: Re: typo in image
item.content:encoded: I noticed this too. Please fix it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So far people have recommended listening to Elliot Smith's Tomorrow Tomorrow, Tool's Lateralus (author's choice), and Moonlight Sonata.

I'd like add my own recommendation: Radiohead's How To Disappear Completely, to maximize crushing existential despair:

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=nZq_jeYsbTs (the nice music video with 5M+ views)

In a little while
I'll be gone
The moment's already passed
Yeah it's gone
And I'm not here
This isn't happening

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: noticed a typo in the banner saying "level 5 with restructions," which i could only notice because this is one of my favorite skips.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was listening to "Moonlight Sonata" while I read this. It was the IDEAL atmosphere.

This is GENIUS!

+1

item.title: typo in image
item.content:encoded: was the banner image supposed to say "restrictions" rather than "restructions"?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The Pattern Screamers here are more “Pattern Whispers” because less than 10 people are currently cognizant of the void.

If more than 10 people knew about the void, then there’d definitely be Screaming.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: glub glub

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Reading this objects description, it doesn't sound that threatening, at least compared to what PS have been hyped up to be. I mean, obviously it's not exactly lock it in a box type of not-threatening but it's containable by a handful of guys, and would drive the people who saw it to unexist themselves, probably limiting the spread. The pattern screamers here aren't so much "Pattern Screamers" as "Pattern Whisperers".

Here's my HC: This object is related to SCP-2470, and both of them are weapons or the result of a weapon. Pattern Screamers were a something that existed in great numbers back maybe a billion or so years ago. They didn't appreciate other life to much and their primary weapon was presumably some kind of meme or cognitohazard. Eventually there was some kind of cosmic war against them using every conceivable weapon. This and SCP-2470 would have been used to erase large amounts of information from existence, possibly including the people who deployed it. The Pattern Screamers were still resilient little cosmic horrors who manage to just barely cling to existence in these situations through sheer anger . The pattern screamer were eventually sent into hiding in (at least in this galaxy) after fighting for millions of years and causing something like 1030 casualties.

Afterwords things like this were left just sitting around for lower krasnov scale civs to wander into, sorta like unexploded ordinance left behind after a world war.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: im going to pour orange juice in it, that's a good idea, it's probably thirsty.

item.title: jesus dude
item.content:encoded:
i should not be reading this at 3 in the godbedamned morning. this is messing me up.

that log did a very good job of convincing me that the log was a guy talking to the perception of somebody who had in actuality entirely ceased to exist between one moment and the next.

luckily though, i just realized that given the hazards involved in trying to transfer the cadavers of those who knew of the void's nature, the most practical solution for the disposal of the remains of the dead is a catapult

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: that would shake the fucking soul outta me lmao

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
this is very clever. building a structure around where nothing isn't happens to be difficult when the perimeter where things meet nothing is that large and you can only have so many people involved, tho. i imagine the foundation could do it, in time, but it'd take a while and add additional risk and honestly the current containment system is adequate.

i mean heck the anomalous phenomenon nearly contains itself, the only reason they dont just forget it exists is because humans being what they are, people will find it and be devoured by it and idk if thats ethically acceptable. and i guess on the practical side of things, in a society where the foundation continues to exist, eventually you're going to run into it with enough people again that youd need to figure out all this bullshit again, so the best thing to do is keep a skeleton crew on the task and try to keep humans in general away from that region.

actually thinking on it now im a little surprised there arent containment procedures to keep a generous perimeter region entirely clear of human presence save for the skeleton crew for the foreseeable longterm. given global warming, i could see there being an issue w that sooner or later lmao.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"History abhors a paradox." - Legacy of Kain.

"Nature doesn't abhor a vacuum but people do." - djkaktus

Raises the question how much distance can you put yourself away from this thing before it considers the area to have too many people and starts the screamers phenomenon.

Because if it were me, I'd have a mechanized device push a long-long stick and start poking at the void's parameters. Given that this phenomenon of perception is only relegated to things capable of pattern recognition, the machine can be built with no cognition and only senses if the stick stops being it's original length or if there's any obstructions to the edge of the stick. Then you trace new sticks around the vacuum to where the void specifically starts and ends. With enough pokes, you can even create 3-D renders based on the data-set you have on locations that make the stick chopped.

Which raises another point… couldn't you build a structure around the 1km diameter based on the surface area the void inhabits based on your poke-research? Assuming this thing doesn't just go on forever into the Earth's crust, you can theoretically (and expensively) map out what is and isn't reality with multiple stick-based research projects.

item.title: So.
item.content:encoded:
Lastly, the effect human perception has on SCP-3930’s perceived properties cannot be diminished with amnestics, or even natural death

So what your saying is… we have brought ghosts in and they failed to deduce the void?

+1 for the amazing concept.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Has anyone tried throwing some clothes at this thing? If I were naked in the middle of the woods and had ten people looking at me, I would be screaming, too.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I had just read the part where the not-D-Class was talking about how if they looked away, they wouldn't perceive him anymore and then that very thing happened, and I happened to look away from my phone right then for just a second, and when I looked back, my browser had crashed.

So, that was a thing.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: When you talk about things that don't want to exist and want to harm us for making them exist, are you talking about these guys?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: An entire SCP file about nothing, yet it's interesting. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I only read the intro without the input credentials. I'll probably just not read anything else. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Perception altered nothing
Perception altered lack of reality
Perception altered void
Perception altered " "

item.title: Admin Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded: Noting that a recent edit by DrHelzPolowski was reverted, as it introduced new content beyond a spelling or grammar fix. The Site Rules indicate that such an edit is not allowed to be made without permission from the original author or staff.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
First off, there's no real way to tell where the boundary of the void is by just looking at it, so you'd have to be real certain your stick is long enough.

As for what happens when you pull it back out, you'd definitely be able to do that, and you'd still have whatever part of the pole hadn't just recently stopped existing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Congrats on 400+!

Genuine question: what happens if you stick an object in, say, a really long pole, but only partway and then try to pull it back out?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
the existence hurts
+1

item.title: A blink at the center of existance
item.content:encoded: This is incredible. Up-vote. What happened to site-13 Faminepulse?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I enjoyed the attempt to explain something that literally doesn't exist. It isn't really a void, because a void is still a thing - an emptiness that exists. This isn't even that, it's a state of total nonbeing.

What I'm trying to wrap my head around here is whether the Screamers are "real", for lack of a better term - things that have found a way to "exist" in a state of nonexistence, and to which the hard lines of our version of reality would be pure pain - or if they're an emergent phenomenon caused by our minds attempting to grapple with literally nothing.

I also like the fact that the cover story for this SCP is the literal truth about it - SCP-3930 does not exist. At all, in any sense. It isn't even a vacuum, because a vacuum is something. This just is not.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That's a good line, but I was actually listening to Tool's Lateralus, specifically the outro:

With my feet upon the ground
I lose myself between the sounds
And open wide to suck it in
I feel it move across my skin
I'm reaching up and reaching out
I'm reaching for the random or
Whatever will bewilder me
Whatever will bewilder me
And following our will and wind
We may just go where no one's been
We'll ride the spiral to the end
And may just go where no one's been
Spiral out, keep going
Spiral out, keep going
Spiral out, keep going
Spiral out, keep going

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
been one of my favorites since I first read it. Makes my skin crawl in the best ways. It does that really hard to pull off thing of comprehensibly describing something not wholly comprehensible, and does it really well.

this might be a stretch, but were you listening to Elliott Smith when writing this, because this feels uncannily relevant:

"I got static in my head
The reflected sound of everything
Tried to go to where it led
But it didn't lead to anything"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good catch; I'm just dumb. Fixed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Dr. Pietrov Kuzkin

Both names are last names. You can change the first one to Piotr for it to sound more plausible.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: love love love this

item.title: Re: Some Theorizing
item.content:encoded:
Oh God why can't I upvote forum comments aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa
This whole SCP-connecting, rabbit hole-descending thing reminds me a lot of the whole On Mount Golgotha canon, and now that I think of it, it may be connected somehow (or I might just be biased because I love that cannon and at first I read "Hanged King" instead of "Scarlet King" and this is all just a massive stretch but o well)

Ok, so as you mentioned, a headcanon exists in which the Daevites were given SCP-1000's technology, and chances are that SCP-1000 used their technology to create (and presumedly destroy, if its true that primitive humans used all their tech to destroy them and then destroyed what they could of that tech) those voids where our minds (and probably the sasquatch's minds too) create the Pattern Screamers. The fact that 3930 is in Siberia, around the same area the Daevite culture formed really gets the noggin joggin.
As you said, most think that it was the Scarlet King the one that gave SCP-1000 tech to the Daevites, which makes sense at first glance since the Daevites were practicioners (if not the founders of) the first Sarkic Cults.

+ show block
– hide block

But there is a little issue there. If tales like Fear Alone (which coincidentally was also written by djkaktus) are to be believed, Sarkic rituals are not done to make the Scarlet King interact with the universe, but to dissuade him to do so. All those fucked up murderrapecannibalism rituals done in his name are to keep him pleased so that he doesn't go all XK on the world, and although the Daevites were murerous lunatics with a taste for sadism and ethnic clensing, they weren't stupid. They wanted to rule the world, not to end it.

So if the Scarlet King didn't hand the Daevites the Bigfoot tech, then who did?
Maybe another king or interdimensional government official. Enter the Hanged King and the Ambassador of Alagadda. We know that the Ambassador interacts with this world, and we know where it enters from, too. We also know that the Ambassador takes enjoyment and fun out of suffering, as we can see the effect it has in 701 plays, and how much it enjoyed the MTF members tearing eachother apart in 2267. What more enjoyable to such a being that a conscience that suffers because of its own existence? Maybe it was for its own amusement that it gave the Daevites the 1000 tech, both to enjoy what they did to the world, and in hopes they created some sweet, perpetually suffering pattern screamers.

Another connection I find is between the pattern screamers and SCP-012 and sure, I know it sounds stupid, since hearing 012 makes you go suicidally insane as soon as you hear it, making you die trying to complete the piece, but there's the catch. Maybe the screams are the complete piece. Maybe the pattern screamers, their hate, are the purest representation of 012. Or maybe its the other way around. Maybe 012 is an attempt at reproducing the screams. Maybe its an attempt at trying to make the human mind comprehend the abyss' sound. We know the Daevites (see the ethnic clensing link) are in some way connected to 012, and the Court of Alagadda too, so maybe this all connects.


Please collapse long posts. ~Zyn

Or maybe I'm insane and sleep deprived since I'm writing this at 1 am, whichever option you prefer.

item.title: Some Theorizing
item.content:encoded:
In SCP-1795, it states that "Pattern Screamers" killed their creators. Now, it's pretty much all but canon that SCP-1795 was built by SCP-1000. That activates the ol' almonds. Maybe SCP-3930 was created by SCP-1000. Maybe the pattern screamers were the reason why humanity got an edge in overthrowing SCP-1000 in the first place. If you accept the headcanon that the reason why the Daevites were so advanced was because the the Scarlet King gave them SCP-1000's technology, the rabbit hole deepens. Is this a creation of the Scarlet King? IIRC, the Scarlet King's whole story is that he hates being alive so much that he wants to take out life itself to get rid of it. Sounds familiar?

The lore deepens if you consider the other "sphere of nothingness" artifacts, like SCP-319. In SCP-319, its creator states that he's found a way into "another world." What if he knew more than us? What if this world is actually the personal realm of the Scarlett King?

Who knows? Maybe this is the hole that SCP-2317 pulls himself out of.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a really great article. The only jarring thing for me is that- through no fault of the author's- the description really reminds me of the House That Doesn't Exist from Welcome to Night Vale. And now I'm imagining the description being read in Cecil's voice.

"SCP-3930 is a stretch of land in Russia which does not actually exist. It looks like it exists- like it's just right there when you look at it- and it's surrounded by similar stretches of land on all sides, so it would make more sense for it to be there than not; nevertheless, the Foundation has assured us that it does not, in fact, exist."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ show block
– hide block

A lot of this is focused on this idea that humans are almost characteristically good at finding patterns where patterns don't exist. People who hear sounds in static, see shapes in clouds, that sort of thing. Our brains are so good at it and it's one of the reasons we've developed the way we have as a species.

So this is just that idea taken to its logical end. The human brain, when faced with an area where there is literally nothing, furiously begins to fill in the blank with something that it understands. For the sake of this article, that something is the Russian wilderness, a convenient backdrop that is already there. The same goes with things that enter the void; it doesn't make sense for them to just not be there, so the mind fills in the gaps.

But this process isn't perfect, just like we're not, so errors begin to form. As more and more of those errors build up, residual consciousness accumulates on the edge of the void. This consciousness isn't real, but it feels real, for both us an the consciousness itself. At small enough values, this consciousness isn't strong enough to draw conclusions about its own quasi existence. But if more begins to build up, and it realizes the paradox of its very being (or lack thereof), it suffers immensely. It begins to scream, and it begins to hate, and it hates nothing more than us. Not only are we the ones who made it real enough to experience this horror, but we're also very real. That envy and that malevolence only makes the hate stronger. Thus, a pattern screamer is born.

So much of this is tied into the sort of djkaktus brand hierarchy of the SCPverse. In 3812 I talk at length about how reality looks as you ascend it, but this (and, to a certain extent, 3000) are about what's at the bottom. Reality rests on an endless void, and between that void and what can be considered real is the hard line between what is, and what isn't. We are what is, and the void (and anything that passes beyond it) and, again, to a certain extent Anantashesha, are what isn't. Pattern screamers are the film that forms between those two things. They neither are, or are not. They're not real enough to self actualize, but not unreal enough so as to not exist and thus not have any agency in the narrative.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

If I've got it right, SCP-3930 isn't the utter void located in Russia (although there is -a- void located in Russia), it's the phenomenon that occurs when humans are very close to such a void. When they are close to it, when they know something has attempted to enter it (and thus, ceased to exist) they are generally incapable of perceiving such, an create vivid shared hallucinations to explain away what's actually in the void.

One mechanism for combating this is to constantly assure the people that this is a void, and what you're seeing/hearing/feeling is just an illusion, which is why the "does not exist" line is repeated so many times. It's not a mimetic effect (I thought it was at first), it's literally true, it's a health and safety measure there to keep the reader from having a void-hallucination/pattern screamer.

What this reminds me of is texture glitches in video games… like when you clip through a wall and you just see random textures flashing all around you.

Like this: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ZBrOfb8huxY&t=0m26s

item.title: Re: Damn
item.content:encoded: Eh. I had an idea about something along these lines as well. It's not that wildly unthinkable a concept that more than one person couldn't come across it. Hiding 'actual' information within collapsibles is done in hundreds of other SCPs, so I have no idea why that would be cited as "evidence". The author already has an established article which uses repetition to great effect, so it's clear that's already something in their wheelhouse. I don't think there's any merit to this. People simply have similar ideas sometimes.

item.title: Re: Damn
item.content:encoded:
I'll start off by apologising for being so public and douchy about my accusation.

I'll leave the details out, and just say that I've contacted staff and we'll leave it to them to decide what is going to happen.

item.title: Re: Damn
item.content:encoded: "Wanting to do something" and "Actually done something" is different. Nobody could ever take an idea out of your head if you haven't done anything with it yet. "The thing you wanted to do", ironically, for all intents and purpose does not exist, thus no one could ever know what it is unless you've stopped "wanting" to do it and actually done it.

item.title: Re: Damn
item.content:encoded:
Echoing kaktus, are you trying to accuse him of plagiarism?

That's one hell of a serious accusation that shouldn't be done lightly.

item.title: Re: Damn
item.content:encoded: Dunno, man. Sometimes that's just the way it goes. Not real keen on the implications here, though.

item.title: Damn
item.content:encoded: Some times I've just gotta learn when to shut up and leave it to staff.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Absolutely adored this. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Kektus, you beautiful bastard +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really liked it until it was Pattern Screamers. It felt pretty wild and unique. Then it started getting explained more, and more, and then it toned to a 'well, it's basically just another thing what cosmics horror.' That's okay. If that's the vision, I'm happy to upvote based on the material rather than the end goal.

item.title: Audio Adaptation
item.content:encoded:
This is my first foray into the world of "Hella long Kaktus adaptations," but the intensity of the opening with all the assertions that SCP-3930 "does not exist" had me gripped from the start. Great job!

https://youtu.be/dwSB2geQVTE
